title: Good watch....affordable price
text:
My Fossil 5 Generation Smartwatch started giving charging problems so I decided to get a new smartwatch.
After doing some research online I decided to get the Amazfit Bip U Pro……… This Amazfit Bip U Pro. has a very sleek design with a minimal and lightweight feel, weighing only 31 grams, plus a large and bright screen for the price.

The face of the watch measures 3.63cm and is rectangular in form. The screen doesn’t take up the whole face, there is a bezel around the whole edge of the watch.
The whole body is made up of polycarbonate and is built well and the display is protected with curved Corning Gorilla Glass 3 for additional protection.
This watch shines when it comes to fitness and health-related features with the inclusion of heart rate, SpO2, sleep, breathing tracking and much more. It comes with a host of workout modes, including running, swimming, cycling, elliptical training and walking.
You can navigate the watch by either using the button on

title: Resets itself/looses data.  HORRIBLE documentation
text:
For some reason, it seems that the $100 price point is the dividing line for Amazfit products being decent/working well, and not. To explain..... I have purchased 3 Amazfit watches, starting with this one. There are issues and disappointments with all of them, however, this particular watch worked OK for about 4 days/nights, then it would randomly reset itself, and in the process delete all data it had accumulated. That was what made me return it.
A couple of very annoying things with this particular watch....
1. SP02 has no automatic feature/setting. Meaning that the only way it will take a reading is if you do it manually.
2. The zepp app that is required with these watches is annoying because each watch displays a bit differently on the app, due to various watches having given functions or not. But the real annoyance is syncing data, which seems to have a mind of its own.... if I try to make it sync sometimes it will, and other times not.
3. DO NOT b

title: SMS test for glass wearers n More
text:
[[VIDEOID:4ee7a9a0c7fcfe4b12038d0b6553470]] PROS:
- Excellent clear, color screen! I was a lil concerned that it was not an Amoled screen, but looks great and vibrant.
- SMS is larger fonts than on my other smart watches n bands and I do not need glasses to read text messages. (On video it is illustrated)
- Alexa works fine. Very convenient on wrist.. So glad you do not need to use the Alexa word to activate because it wud set off machines in my house.
- Music and Photo Shutter controls works as described.
-Perfect size for a woman's thin wrist at 1.4".
-Vibration is strong n you do not miss an alert.
- Just got it on an Amazon sale.
CONS:
- Setup was horribly, took numerous attempts on Zepp. Works great now.
- 9 day battery is a lil suspect. Still testing.
- 4 watch faces sucks when u have to keep 2 of ice installed ones when customizing faces and are forced to delete one of the two personalized faces you created.
- Sometimes

title: THE BEST Smartwatch out there with GPS & OTHER Great Features for the Buck!
text: I love Amazfit Watches.  I've tried many other brands of smart watches, but I must admit, that Amazfit offers the BEST watch, with the most useful features - for far less than other Smart Watch manufacturers.  I was using the Amazfit Bip S watch as my daily Smartwatch and thought that I couldn't find a better watch.  Then, Amazfit came out with the BIP U PRO!  The BIP U PRO is truly a winner 🥇!  Slightly larger screen, gorgeous screen, plenty of beautiful and totally free watch faces, 7 to 8 day battery 🔋 life before recharging, built in GPS, ALEXA (with Mic) in the watch - that will interface with your cellphone and show you instant info from Alexa on your BIP U PRO SCREEN.  This watch has so many useful features:  Accurate Fitness Tracker with over 60bFitness Activities , Heart Rate Monitor, Blood Oxygen, Sleep Monitor.  The other 2 cool really useful features are:  Music Control (Control the music volume, and skip tracks - from your Amazfit BIP U PRO - that is playing through your c

title: Missing GPS Function?
text: I would give this watch a higher rating except I can't figure out where the GPS functionality is. My model says A2008 Amazfit Bip U Pro, the model number in the specs shows W2008OV1N. Did they send the wrong model? All features appear to be there except the GPS, which is shown in the product description, in the features, and in the product reviews. Wondering if I got the wrong product... Can't find a way to contact the manufacturer to have them check serial number and give me the true product number. SO, just confused. Other features are great...

title: Band hurts wrist badly
text: AMAZON SHOULD NOT ALLOW THIS PRODUCT TO BE SOLD!!!

title: Good deal.  I like the face-display choices.
text:
For the money, this is very useful. First,it is an accurate watch that I don't have to reset. It tells me the battery status, time, day and date without having to scroll. But what I really like it that it shows who is calling my iPhone without having to pull the phone out of it's holster when I am shopping. Also, if I receive a message, it shows the message.
On the down side, I can't get it to display my all-day pulse rate in graph form on my computer. Same with my night time sleep stages. Doesn't display on my PC in graph form. It will show a "skyscraper" type sleep display on my iPhone, but it isn't as good as my Fitbit display on my PC. Oh, and the battery lasts much longer than my Fitbit.

Follow up after two weeks:

After wearing the watch for a while, the skin on my wrist, under the watch band, was red and peeling. One other buyer on this list had the same problem. I replaced the band with one of the “Barton” brands, listed on Amazon. It has a

title: Super reasonable price for a feature laden watch
text: First I didn't put any stars in the mobile app line above as I'm not really sure what was being asked. I use this with my phone all the time and the sync seems perfect. As mentioned this was very reasonably priced and has given me really every feature I was hoping for. Watch is comfortable, charges quickly but goes for days without charging. It has completely opened my eyes when it comes to my sleep patterns. Very interesting. The watch comes with a number of nice looking faces and I've found a couple that suit my needs perfectly. I had previously bought a lower cost watch –still $35 more than this one. It started to malfunction very quickly. Sure wish I'd come across this one first. I'd gladly buy again and have considered getting a more expensive model from this brand as they have some really good looking ones.

title: Would not connect to my Samsung S10 Ultra. The Software and Instructions are poor at best.
text: Would not connect to my Samsung S10 Ultra. The Software for the phone is not flexible.  Instructions are almost non-existent and the online help is poor.  The phone number for in person phone service never answers and when you leave a message to call you they don't.

title: Cost-Effective and Reliable Alternative to Apple Watch
text:
We were looking to get our son a watch that would work along with the iPhone we got him last summer (which he has taken good care of), but I just wasn't ready to invest in yet another expensive gadget for him given how active he is. After some research and looking at a few recommendations, this AmazFit Bip U Pro SmartWatch seemed to fit the bill.
Our son is a very active 6th grader and trust me when I say that he has put this watch through its trials. From rec football practice, to swim team, rough-housing with the neighborhood kids, I was almost certain this watch would give up. Boy was I wrong. This thing is running like a champ.
The Bip U Pro SmartWatch has a lot of the same functions as the Apple Watch, and best of all, it syncs with my son's iPhone SE and alerts him to text messages, emails, among other things. It also helped him find his phone here in the house when little sister hid it under the couch. :)
I would definitely recommend this for any parent getting as

title: If you want/need O2 measurements - don't buy
text:
My Cardio Doctors want me top watch my O2 and Heart Rate at 3 hour intervals as a part of a Senior Cardio Study at Duke. I had seen Smart Watch claims of Heart Rate and O2 Monitor Capability. This is the 2nd Smart Watch I've tried. If O2 monitoring with a Smart Watch is your goal, don't' bother.

Heart rate was occasionally he same, but occasionally 4 or more off, as both of my Finger Oximeters and my Bluetooth Blood Pressure/Heart Rate Monitor, but O2 on the "Bip U Pro" was a waste of time. It frequently read 99% and other times was in the 80's.

If O2 monitoring is among your goals, don't bother with a smart watch. Duke Cardio folks echo the same opinion re O2 monitoring with ANY Smart Watch.

title: A Good Intro Fitness Tracker (B rating) *Not a Smart Watch*
text:
I have been using this fitness tracker watch for about a month now and I give it a B rating for quality and function. It gets about 7 days of use before I need to charge it and easily synch with my iPhone for recording data/updates. The silicone band is comfortable and doesn't cause me irritation or noticeable as I wear it all day. The waterproof is good for showering, swimming, and I have free dove down to about 20 ft with no problems or issues. I am 6'2" and about 220 lbs and it fits my wrist just fine,

Issues:
- The watch face scratches very easily (get a screen protector)
- If your arm isn't moving it won't count steps. (Hands in pockets or pushing a shopping cart equals no steps)
-I have been standing at my desk for a half-hour, walking around the office, and the watch will say I need to stand and move around.
- Display is hard to read with some of the face options. Whish I could customize the things to show and were on the screen.
- Limite

title: does what it's supposed to
text:
*edit: I've found a con and it bugged me enough to update my review. If your phone is on "Do not disturb" and you leave your phone on at night for emergency calls, the watch still vibrates with every text, regardless of whether they're on your emergency list. This is annoying when you're a light sleeper and you get a text for go cart racing or Dairy Queen at 4 am. I'm assuming it would still go off with a call as well.

I have a Garmin Vivofit that for some reason the notification vibrations will work for a couple months, then stop working for a while, then randomly start working again. I was sick of this, and went looking for something not too expensive and found this. I'm still getting used to it, but it vibrates when I get a call or text, so I'm happy so far. The band is very flexible and comfortable, which is nice, since my Vivofit band was a little stiff.

title: Total waste of money
text:
This watch is inexpensive compared to others and when I first got it it worked pretty good the one and only problem was the spo2 did not read at all. But in the last month or so it has become unusable I wrote to them as seen below
with all the updates you sent lately you have ruined this watch beyond compare. nothing is working correctly anymore. it does not measure your sleep correctly it does not measure calories burned during an activity like walking. the spo2 has never worked since the day I got it. even the PAI is erratic. I COUNT ON THIS WATCH TO GIVE ME INCENTIVE TO KEEP GOING BUT NOW IT'S GIVING ME NOTHING BUT DEPRESSION BECAUSE I DON'T SEE CALORIES BEING BURNED AND OTHER THINGS NOT WORKING MAYBE I SHOULD CHARGE YOU FOR MY PSYCHIATRIST BILL.

title: Not a good choice for a fitness watch
text: Calorie consumption, PAI score, etc are all based off of pulse rate. This device constantly looses the heart rate, mainly when exercising. I’ve tried adjusting the band fit to no prevail. Even when doing a manual heart rate, I often get no results. If you want a waterproof watch that has a customizable screen as well as long battery life and fast charging, this may be for you. If your looking for a true fitness watch, I suggest to look elsewhere.

title: Fine watch, but zero customer service
text: The watch is great, when it works, which is 99% of the time. However, if you ever have problems with the watch, don't expect any help from the manufacturer. There is a customer support form on Amazfit's website, but it seems to be just for show because they never respond.

title: Cheap feeling
text: I couldn't get past how cheap the watch felt. Pretty color and navigation was super easy. My daughter liked it. I've just been comparing watches to the Fitbit Sense which feels high end. This just didn't.

title: Good quality for the money
text:
I've had this watch for about two weeks now and like it. The strap is comfortable and there are more than a dozen watch faces to choose. It took a couple of days to get used to how to operate the watch (finding the workouts and finding where to change the brightness) but now it is second nature.

The step count seems to be accurate as is the heart rate. I haven't used the sleep function so I cannot comment on that.

The best thing is that it has a strap with a buckle rather than the type of strap with the post that snaps into a hole and that always seems to come loose.

All in all, a good quality watch of this type.

title: Great watch for the price.
text: After buying the earlier brands of Fitbit which I loved. I bought myself a Luxe on sale for $100 last December 2021. It didn’t record anything accurately and just didn’t function the way it should. CS was if no help. I switched to Amazfit!  This is a great watch, the battery has lasted over a week. It accurately tells time, my heart rate, steps and I love this watch. It’s great and is so light on my wrist!!

title: Terrible smart watch, inaccurate and waste of time/money
text: After 5 months of use I’m done with this. It seems to be getting less accurate every workout. It can’t even get step counts correct. The accuracy of the heart beat monitoring varies too. The sleep monitor is often wrong about awake times so I don’t believe the other sleep data either. The only thing I can believe with this watch is the time of day.

title: Good budget watch if you don't need always-on display
text: Overall, this is a very reasonably priced watch with a lot of functionality. However, the fact that it doesn't have the always-on screen option was a deal breaker for me and I had to replace it with one of their other models.

title: It can’t be set up
text: Got this and have tried for several hours to download the app and send this up. You can never read the verification code to input it so this is garbage. Will send it back.

title: Features - 10; Accuracy - 7
text:
This is my second Amazfit watch and quite an upgrade over the previous one. The expanded list of sports for the Workout History is kind of fun. It even has curling, which is my main winter athletic activity. PAI was a new concept to me. Surprisingly it's the one that motivates me the most. One thing I noticed however is that the step count is about 7% below my actual step range. Also, sometimes if you don't actively say you have a sporting activity going you can still lose PAI.

The major downside of this watch compared to my old one is that all these fancy features have resulted in a relatively short battery life. I still recommend the watch as a good value for the price but know what the shortcomings are.

title: Smart Fitbit
text: While this is technically a watch, I would classify it as more of a smart Fitbit. It does alert you of calls and messages, but you cannot accept, decline, or answer calls or messages from the watch. The price was fantastic, I just should have read the capabilities a little more closely. Works great, battery life great, app easy to use. Band does look cheap.

title: Time after time
text:
This is an amazing tracker at this price point. I got this to replace my six year old Samsung gear fit 2. I'm super happy with the battery life on the Amazfit. Charged it 5 days ago and still at 76%. I'll update later with awesome features.
Updated 1/12/2022
Added a picture of the watch with one of my favorite features. You can take a photo from your gallery and add it to one of four watch faces. Depending on your picture, you can have the time on either side, top or bottom of your pictures' focal point. Another great feature is alarm, and the main reason I purchased this watch. I replaced a Samsung Gear Fit II with this one. It has a lot more features and better interface with the Zepp app.

title: Junk, Dated, Unreliable
text: When you sweat and your HR is up and you need this thing the most it comes up very short. If you get sweat on the face it goes haywire. Very dated gadget. Pay a little more and get something much better. I am getting a charge 5

title: Love the GPS not the ECG
text:
Update: lowering to 3 stars. ALL the units need to be changeable to imperial and NOT default to metric for this watch to be useful in the USA!

Bought two. One for the ECG function for my husband but cannot get that function to work. The prompt reply with instructions from the manufacturer only measures the heart rate and does not apply to the ECG graph.
Good points: Love the GPS function for walking. Love changing the watch faces, just wish there were more serene or scenic choices. Love the sleep tracking.
Hiccups:
Blood glucose: what is mmol? OK I figured it out but what use is it if you want people to use it and not have to get out their calculators to decipher the results? My diabetic husband for sure will not use this “feature”.
Body temp: It is only manually entered and only in Celsius not Fahrenheit. It translates it to F on the chart after you enter in C. No option to select your preference under Profile/Settings/Units.
Walking: As I am walk

title: Update 04-10-2022
text:
I decided to increase the review rating to four stars because of the excellent and very fast customer service. I had an issue with the watch not working and within a few days the CS department sent me a return label, which in my experience is what good customer service is all about. In the end i did not need it as the fault was the charging lead and not the watch. I had a charger that i use for my iPhone and that works on this watch as well.

I had a much cheaper smart watch that worked very well but the strap snapped and i was unable to get a replacement due to its size and shape. I did some extensive research online and decided to buy this Amazfit due to the reviews. Sadly the watch does not quite live up to the hype. Although the battery life started off around 9 days its now down to 5 days with the same use. The get up and exercise reminder is a bit of a joke as it's really just a timer, you could be running a marathon and it will still remind you to get up and exercise

title: Lots of features for a low price
text: I bought this to replace my older Amazfit fit watch. I especially like all of the fitness and sleep data that it makes available. The battery has to be charged much more often than the previous Amazfit watch, which is annoying. You have to pay attention to the battery life indicator or it will run out when you least expect it. Some features on the watch quit working pretty soon - the O2 measure, the resting heart rate.

title: Good watch with functional O2 sensor
text: Nice watch, lots of faces available. Wish I didn’t have to push the button to see time.

title: Accidental Pauses
text: I bought this mainly to track my exercise, however it is very easy to accidentally pause or turn off the tracking when in exercise mode, with movements of the wrist. This means it actually isn't tracking activity, when you think it is. I really don't have the opportunity to constantly check my watch while exercising.

title: Broke after 6 months
text: Watch was fine, at first. The watch is now 7th months old, and is no longer working. Even inexpensive watches, should last for at least a year.

title: Double check the heart rate reading with the real heart rate monitor.
text: Worked as expected. Compared the heat rate with the medical one and it was reading that my heart was fine but the medical showed that it was very high... The reading measurements are showing lower than the real heart monitor instrument.  So always double check  your readings.
